data.title: TGS ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• TGS ASA press release (OTCQX:TGSNF): Q3 revenues of USD 388 million, a 26% sequential growth.
  • Order inflow of USD 436 million during Q3 2025 – total order backlog of USD 473 million
  • Strong cash flow reducing net debt to USD 432 million, compared to USD 479 million at the end of Q2 2025
  • Solid balance sheet allows for stable dividend payment of USD 0.155 per share to be paid in Q4 2025
  • Continued scrutiny of cash outflow - capex guidance for 2025 reduced to USD 110 million from USD 135 million

data.title: Sandisk leads gain in memory, storage names as Samsung, SK hynix likely to boost prices
data.description:
• Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) led gains in memory and storage stocks on Thursday after it was reported that Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK hynix(OTCPK:HXSCF) are likely to boost prices on memory.
  • The South Korean technology companies have raised their prices on dynamic random access memory and NAND flash by up to 30% for the fourth-quarter, Korean Economic Daily reported. The steep price hike is due to the surging demand for all things related to artificial intelligence, which has caused a supply shortage in both memory and storage.
  • Seagate shares rose more than 3% on Thursday, as did competitor Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC). SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK), which was spun off from Western Digital to focus on its flash memory business, rose more than 8.5%, while Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) tacked on nearly 1.5%.
  • Seagate, Micron, Sandisk and Western Digital did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Azelis Group NV reports 9M results
data.description:
• Azelis Group NV press release (OTCPK:AZLGF): Nine-month revenue of EUR 3.2B, representing a 0.8% decline over the prior year (+2.1% on a constant currency basis), driven by a 0.6% organic decline and a 3.0% FX headwind, offset by a 2.7% revenue growth contribution from acquisitions.

  • Adjusted EBITA of EUR 332.6m results in adjusted EBITA margin of 10.5%, and conversion margin of 44.2%.

data.title: Union Pacific exceeds expectations for Q3 profit, operating ratio
data.description:
Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) reported a profit beat and an operating ratio improvement of more than 100 basis points in the third quarter, which overshadowed the railroad operator's slight revenue miss.

Shares of the company were marginally lower on the NYSE after markets opened.

Operating revenue in Q3 grew 3%, driven by solid core pricing gains, partially offset by lower fuel surcharge, the company said.

The quarterly operating ratio was 59.2%, an improvement of 110 bps, and ahead of the consensus estimate of 58.7%. The adjusted operating ratio improved by 180 bps to 58.5%.

Locomotive productivity improved 4% to 140 gross ton-miles per horsepower day, while workforce productivity improved 6% to 1,165 car miles per employee in Q3.

Net income for the three months ended September 30 was $1.79B, or $3.01 per share, compared with $1.67B, or $2.75 per share, for the same period last year.

On a per-share basis, the Omaha, Nebraska-based company earned $3.08 per share, beating the consensus estimate by 8 cents.

Total revenue was $6.24B in Q3, missing the average analyst estimate by only $10M.

For 2025, it continues to expect pricing dollars to be accretive to the operating ratio and EPS growth of high single to low double digits. Capital spending of $3.4B is unchanged, while buybacks are paused for the Norfolk Southern merger.

data.title: Bureau Veritas SA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Bureau Veritas SA press release (OTCPK:BVRDF): Q3 Revenue of EUR1.58M.
  • Acceleration of M&A programs with two transactions signed in October for a total annualized revenue of c. EUR 32 million in line with the LEAP | 28 portfolio strategy: the first one to expand leadership in the B&I division in Europe, and the second to create new strongholds in the Renewables space. Eight acquisitions signed or closed year-to-date adding EUR 92 million of annualized revenue› Completion of a EUR 700 million bond issuance carrying a coupon of 3.375% with maturity in October 2033, and rated A3 by Moody’s.

  • 2025 Outlook confirmed

    Based on the 9-month performance, leveraging a robust opportunities pipeline, a solid backlog, and mid-to-long-term strong market fundamentals, Bureau Veritas reaffirms its outlook for the full year 2025:

    › Mid-to-high single-digit organic revenue growth,› Improvement in adjusted operating margin at constant exchange rates,› Strong cash flow, with a cash conversion2 above 90%.

data.title: Swedish Orphan Biovitrum AB reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Swedish Orphan Biovitrum AB press release (OTCPK:BIOVF): Q3 Total revenue increased 13 percent, 21 percent at constant exchange rates (CER), to SEK 7,776 M (6,894)
  • The adjusted EBITAmargin was 47 percent (43), excluding IAC of SEK -6,664 M. EBITA was SEK 3,620 M (2,923), corresponding to a margin of 47 percent (42).
  • EBIT was SEK -3,858 M (2,038) including the impairment of Vonjo by SEK 6,612 M.
  • Earnings per share before dilution was SEK -8.40 (4.27) and EPS after dilution was SEK -8.32 (4.22).
  • Adjusted EPS before dilution was SEK 6.11 (4.36) and adjusted EPS after dilution was SEK 6.05 (4.31).
  • Cash flow from operating activities was SEK 1,840 M (1,201)
  • Outlook 2025 - updated
  • Revenue is anticipated to grow at low double-digit percentage at CER(previously high-single-digit)
  • Adjusted EBITA margin adjusted is anticipated to be at mid-to-high 30s percentage of revenue(previously mid-30s)

data.title: Paradox Interactive reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Paradox Interactive press release (OTCPK:PRXXF): Q3 Revenues amounted to MSEK 395.0 (MSEK 434.0), a decrease of 9% compared to the same period last year.
  • Operating profit amounted to MSEK 111.8 (MSEK 142.8), a decrease of 22%.
  • Profit after financial items amounted to MSEK 116.3 (MSEK 151.2), and profit after tax amounted to MSEK 92.9 (MSEK 119.7).
  • Cash flow from operating activities amounted to MSEK 195.2 (MSEK 153.7), and cash flow from investing activities amounted to MSEK -182.4 (MSEK -115.3).
  • Cash flow from financing activities amounted to MSEK -9.8 (MSEK -9.6).
  • At the end of the period, cash and cash equivalents amounted to MSEK 1,020.4 (MSEK 1,190.4).
  • Earnings per share before dilution amounted to SEK 0.88 (SEK 1.13) and after dilution to SEK 0.88 (SEK 1.13).

data.title: Textron shares fall as quarterly revenue misses estimates
data.description:
Textron (NYSE:TXT) shares fell 4% as regular trading opened Thursday to a seven-week low after the company reported third-quarter results that topped Wall Street’s profit forecasts but slightly missed expectations for revenue.

The Providence, R.I.-based industrial conglomerate posted earnings from continuing operations of $1.31 a share, up from $1.18 in the same quarter last year. Adjusted earnings came in at $1.55 a share, beating analyst estimates for $1.46 a share.

Revenue rose 5% to $3.6 billion, or $175 million higher than a year ago, but came in just shy of consensus expectations for $3.7 billion. The company attributed the gains to stronger performance in its Aviation, Bell and Textron Systems segments, offset partly by softness in its industrial unit following the divestiture of its Powersports business.

Segment results

Textron Aviation sales climbed 10% to $1.5 billion, driven by higher jet and turboprop deliveries, while segment profit jumped $51 million to $179 million. Bell’s revenue also rose 10% to $1.0 billion, benefiting from military programs such as the U.S. Army’s MV-75, though commercial helicopter deliveries declined to 30 from 44 a year earlier.

Textron Systems revenue increased modestly to $307 million, supported by the Ship-to-Shore Connector program, while industrial revenue fell 9% to $761 million on the Powersports divestiture.

The company’s backlog expanded by $2.2 billion to a total of $19.1 billion, led by strong orders at Bell and Textron Systems.

Cash flow from manufacturing operations rose sharply to $281 million, compared with $147 million a year earlier. Textron (NYSE:TXT) returned $206 million to shareholders through buybacks in the quarter, bringing the year-to-date total to $635 million.

For 2025, Textron (TXT) reaffirmed its earnings outlook of $5.19 to $5.39 per share, or $6.00 to $6.20 on an adjusted basis, and expects manufacturing cash flow before pension contributions between $900 million and $1 billion.

data.title: Adobe weighed $3B purchase of AI startup Synthesia - report
data.description:
Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) explored the acquisition of AI video startup Synthesia in recent months for about $3 billion.

Synthesia, a London-based maker of AI software that generates video avatars of people, and Adobe failed to reach a deal after a disagreement over price, according to a report from The Information  on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The talks came after Adobe invested $100 million in Synthesia in April. The $3 billion price tag exceeded the $2.1 billion valuation the AI startup was valued at in a January financing, the report said. 

Synthesia also discussed a potential sale to Meta Platforms (META) earlier this year, though the talks didn't advance, according to the report. 

Synthesia is now looking to raise more venture funding before the end of the year and the value is likely to exceed the $3 billion price discussed with Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE).
